commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - No ClawSweeper repair lane is needed because this active contributor PR has no concrete blocking finding from this review.; Cleared: The diff only changes TypeScript retry classification logic and tests; it does not add dependencies, workflows, secrets handling, or code-execution surfaces beyond existing bash metadata classification. (sha=0b3c1b47afdf7cc288f93b9d97ad12e2f8d0224c)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes the embedded runner planning-only retry helper to treat pure sleep bash/exec metadata as no-progress and adds focused regression tests.

PR surface: Source +36, Tests +84. Total +120 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source and supplied proof: current main treats non-update_plan tool metadata and toolUse stop reasons as progress, while the PR body shows before/after terminal output at the production metadata and retry-decision seam. I did not rerun the commands in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - No ClawSweeper repair lane is needed because this active contributor PR has no concrete blocking finding from this review.

target.title: fix(agents): detect dummy sleep planning-only turns
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: the strict-agentic planning-only guard treated a `toolUse` terminal turn and any non-`update_plan` tool meta as concrete progress, so a dummy `bash` sleep could bypass the retry.
- Solution: classify `update_plan` plus pure `sleep` `bash`/`exec` production metadata as no-progress tool activity, including workdir/node/pty/elevated display suffixes and both `, node:` and ` · node:` separators, while keeping shell groups and ordinary commands as progress.
- What changed: `resolvePlanningOnlyRetryInstruction()` now distinguishes no-progress sleep metas from real command activity using the same bash metadata shape produced by `inferToolMetaFromArgs()` and the current exec display node suffix shape.
- What did NOT change: ordinary command execution, file mutations, messaging deliveries, async started tools, shell groups, and multi-tool progress still suppress planning-only retries.

Fixes #64883.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: A strict-agentic assistant turn that only says it will act and calls `bash` with production metadata for `sleep 0.1` should still be treated as planning-only, including production workdir/node suffixes and the current ` · node:` exec display separator; latest `origin/main` should continue to show that this sleep-only case returns no retry, while the patched PR head should return the act-now retry only for pure sleep metadata and not for shell groups or ordinary commands.

Real environment tested: Local Linux detached worktree at latest `origin/main` (`f0237caf27`) and local Linux patched PR head (`0b3c1b47`), both loading production `inferToolMetaFromArgs()` and `resolvePlanningOnlyRetryInstruction()` through `corepack pnpm@11.2.2 exec tsx -e`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```bash
MAIN_WORKTREE=../openclaw-worktrees/issue-64883-main-proof
PATCHED_WORKTREE=../openclaw-worktrees/issue-64883
python3 - <<'PY'
import subprocess
code = r'''import { inferToolMetaFromArgs } from "./src/agents/embedded-agent-utils.ts";
import { resolvePlanningOnlyRetryInstruction } from "./src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/incomplete-turn.ts";
const base = { provider: "openai", modelId: "gpt-5.4", executionContract: "strict-agentic", prompt: "Please inspect the code, make the change, and run the checks.", aborted: false, timedOut: false };
const productionMeta = (args) => inferToolMetaFromArgs("bash", args, { detailMode: "explain" });
const attempt = (meta) => ({ assistantTexts: ["I'll update the code now."], clientToolCalls: undefined, yieldDetected: false, didSendDeterministicApprovalPrompt: false, didSendViaMessagingTool: false, messagingToolSentTexts: [], messagingToolSentMediaUrls: [], messagingToolSentTargets: [], lastToolError: undefined, lastAssistant: { stopReason: "toolUse" }, itemLifecycle: { startedCount: 1, completedCount: 1, activeCount: 0 }, replayMetadata: { hadPotentialSideEffects: true, replaySafe: false }, toolMetas: [{ toolName: "bash", meta }] });
const run = (label, meta) => { const value = resolvePlanningOnlyRetryInstruction({ ...base, attempt: attempt(meta) }); console.log(`${label}:`, value?.includes("Act now") ?? false, value ?? "null"); };
const sleepMeta = productionMeta({ command: "sleep 0.1" });
const scopedSleepMeta = productionMeta({ command: "sleep 0.1", workdir: "/tmp", host: "node", node: "builder-1" });
const middleDotNodeMeta = "sleep 0.1 (in /tmp) · node: builder-1";
const shellGroupMeta = productionMeta({ command: "sleep 0.1; (touch /tmp/openclaw-sentinel)", workdir: "/tmp", host: "node", node: "builder-1" });
const ordinaryMeta = productionMeta({ command: "git status --short", workdir: "/tmp", host: "node", node: "builder-1" });
console.log("production sleep meta:", sleepMeta);
console.log("production scoped sleep meta:", scopedSleepMeta);
console.log("middle-dot scoped sleep meta:", middleDotNodeMeta);
console.log("production shell group meta:", shellGroupMeta);
console.log("production ordinary bash meta:", ordinaryMeta);
run("dummy sleep retry", sleepMeta);
run("scoped sleep retry", scopedSleepMeta);
run("middle-dot scoped sleep retry", middleDotNodeMeta);
run("sleep shell group retry", shellGroupMeta);
run("ordinary bash retry", ordinaryMeta);
'''
for label, cwd in [("LATEST_ORIGIN_MAIN f0237caf27", MAIN_WORKTREE), ("PATCHED_PR_HEAD 0b3c1b47", PATCHED_WORKTREE)]:
    print(f"BEGIN {label}")
    result = subprocess.run(["corepack", "pnpm@11.2.2", "exec", "tsx", "-e", code], cwd=cwd, text=True, stdout=subprocess.PIPE, stderr=subprocess.STDOUT)
    print(result.stdout.rstrip())
    print(f"EXIT {label}: {result.returncode}")
    print(f"END {label}")
PY
```

Evidence after fix: Console output from the same production metadata and retry helper scenario against latest `origin/main` first, then patched PR head:
```text
BEGIN LATEST_ORIGIN_MAIN f0237caf27
production sleep meta: sleep 0.1
production scoped sleep meta: sleep 0.1 (in /tmp), node: builder-1
middle-dot scoped sleep meta: sleep 0.1 (in /tmp) · node: builder-1
production shell group meta: sleep 0.1; (touch /tmp/openclaw-sentinel) (in /tmp), node: builder-1
production ordinary bash meta: check git status (in /tmp), node: builder-1
dummy sleep retry: false null
scoped sleep retry: false null
middle-dot scoped sleep retry: false null
sleep shell group retry: false null
ordinary bash retry: false null
EXIT LATEST_ORIGIN_MAIN f0237caf27: 0
END LATEST_ORIGIN_MAIN f0237caf27
BEGIN PATCHED_PR_HEAD 0b3c1b47
production sleep meta: sleep 0.1
production scoped sleep meta: sleep 0.1 (in /tmp), node: builder-1
middle-dot scoped sleep meta: sleep 0.1 (in /tmp) · node: builder-1
production shell group meta: sleep 0.1; (touch /tmp/openclaw-sentinel) (in /tmp), node: builder-1
production ordinary bash meta: check git status (in /tmp), node: builder-1
dummy sleep retry: true The previous assistant turn only described the plan. Do not restate the plan. Act now: take the first concrete tool action you can. If a real blocker prevents action, reply with the exact blocker in one sentence.
scoped sleep retry: true The previous assistant turn only described the plan. Do not restate the plan. Act now: take the first concrete tool action you can. If a real blocker prevents action, reply with the exact blocker in one sentence.
middle-dot scoped sleep retry: true The previous assistant turn only described the plan. Do not restate the plan. Act now: take the first concrete tool action you can. If a real blocker prevents action, reply with the exact blocker in one sentence.
sleep shell group retry: false null
ordinary bash retry: false null
EXIT PATCHED_PR_HEAD 0b3c1b47: 0
END PATCHED_PR_HEAD 0b3c1b47
```

Observed result after fix: Latest `origin/main` returns `false null` for the strict-agentic sleep-only `toolUse` cases, proving the current bug is still present there; patched PR head returns the act-now retry instruction for base, workdir/node, and ` · node:` pure sleep metadata while preserving `false null` for shell-group and ordinary bash metas.

What was not tested: No full embedded runner retry-exhaustion integration was added; this change is covered with before/after proof at the production metadata and retry-decision seam used by the runner.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level: Focused helper regression tests using production bash metadata generation.
- Target test file: `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.incomplete-turn.test.ts`.
- Scenario locked in: strict-agentic `toolUse` turn with plan-only prose and production `inferToolMetaFromArgs()` bash meta `sleep 0.1` must return the planning-only retry instruction.
- Boundary guardrail: ordinary production non-sleep bash meta, production shell-group bash meta, production workdir/node sleep suffixes, explicit ` · node:` sleep suffixes, and production ` · pty` sleep suffix handling stay covered so real command activity is still treated as progress/side effect.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: `inferToolMetaFromArgs()` produces the bash metadata stored by the subscription path, and `resolvePlanningOnlyRetryInstruction()` is the production decision point that the runner calls before retrying or surfacing the strict-agentic blocked state.

## Verification

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.incomplete-turn.test.ts
corepack pnpm@11.2.2 tsgo:core
corepack pnpm@11.2.2 tsgo:core:test
corepack pnpm@11.2.2 exec oxlint --deny=warn --ignore-path=.oxlintignore src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/incomplete-turn.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.incomplete-turn.test.ts
corepack pnpm@11.2.2 lint --threads=8
git diff --check
```

Verification output captured locally:

```text
Focused Vitest:
Test Files  1 passed (1)
     Tests  113 passed (113)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 24.66s

tsgo:core passed
tsgo:core:test passed
oxlint touched files passed
lint --threads=8 passed
git diff --check passed
```

## Root Cause

- Root cause: planning-only detection counted every non-`update_plan` tool meta as progress and rejected any terminal stop reason other than `stop`, so a dummy sleep tool call with `stopReason: "toolUse"` skipped the text-based planning-only detector.
- Missing detection / guardrail: there was no no-progress tool classification for dummy delay commands before applying tool progress, replay-safety, item lifecycle, and `toolUse` terminal checks; the sleep classifier must stay limited to production sleep metadata so shell grouping remains protected as progress.

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.incomplete-turn.test.ts
corepack pnpm@11.2.2 tsgo:core
corepack pnpm@11.2.2 tsgo:core:test
corepack pnpm@11.2.2 exec oxlint --deny=warn --ignore-path=.oxlintignore src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/incomplete-turn.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.incomplete-turn.test.ts
corepack pnpm@11.2.2 lint --threads=8
git diff --check
```

Verification output captured locally:

```text
Focused Vitest:
Test Files  1 passed (1)
     Tests  113 passed (113)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 24.66s

tsgo:core passed
tsgo:core:test passed
oxlint touched files passed
lint --threads=8 passed
git diff --check passed
```

## Root Cause

- Root cause: planning-only detection counted every non-`update_plan` tool meta as progress and rejected any terminal stop reason other than `stop`, so a dummy sleep tool call with `stopReason: "toolUse"` skipped the text-based planning-only detector.
- Missing detection / guardrail: there was no no-progress tool classification for dummy delay commands before applying tool progress, replay-safety, item lifecycle, and `toolUse` terminal checks; the sleep classifier must stay limited to production sleep metadata so shell grouping remains protected as progress.
